page: UpCloud HICPU-64xCPU-128GB is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, utilizing a shared CPU allocation. The system is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 300 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU hardware is present on this configuration.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-threaded capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU benchmarks falling into the bottom 10%. However, multi-threaded execution is highly capable, as evidenced by strong, top-25% results in Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics, as well as multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench software compilation and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average range, while Redis SET operations demonstrate strong throughput at 74.26 million operations per second.

The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is centered on high-density multi-core compute workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores or massive per-core memory allocations. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio presents a tradeoff: while highly efficient for parallelized tasks like software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and ray tracing, it is less suitable for single-threaded applications or memory-intensive databases. It is well-suited for parallel processing, CI/CD build pipelines, and high-throughput key-value caching, but less optimal for large LLM text generation or single-core execution.
description: UpCloud HICPU-64xCPU-128GB is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 64 physical cores on an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz with shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and a bundled 300 GB local SSD, with no GPU acceleration. While single-core benchmarks are in the bottom 10%, the instance delivers strong multi-threaded performance, scoring in the top 25% for Passmark CPU suites, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and software compilation. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient solution for highly parallelized workloads, such as CI/CD pipelines, ray tracing, and Redis caching, though the shared CPU allocation and lower single-core speeds represent a tradeoff for single-threaded or dedicated-core requirements.
og_description: UpCloud HICPU-64xCPU-128GB is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 64 AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 300 GB SSD, delivering strong multi-threaded performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud HICPU-64xCPU-128GB is a KVM-based server with 64 AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 300 GB SSD, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core-count compute instance featuring 64 AMD EPYC cores and 128 GB RAM, optimized for parallel processing and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 AMD EPYC 7542 physical cores on x86_64 architecture
128.0 GB RAM (2.0 GB per core) and 300 GB local SSD
Strong multi-threaded performance in compilation and cryptography
Shared CPU allocation under KVM hypervisor
Best suited for parallel processing, CI/CD pipelines, and Redis caching
